channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Concern Regarding Poor Maintenance and Safety of Local Road, 3rd May
item.description:
Dear Sir/Madam, I am writing to raise a concern regarding the condition of a small road/pathway in our area, which is currently not being properly maintained. The area is often overgrown with grass, and there is a noticeable presence of litter, including broken glass bottles. This creates an unpleasant environment and, more importantly, poses a safety risk—particularly during the night, when visibility is low and it becomes very difficult to walk through safely. While occasional grass cutting has been carried out, it is clearly not sufficient. What is needed is a more thorough and consistent maintenance plan, including proper cleaning, removal of hazardous waste, and overall improvement of the pathway. A more comprehensive requalification of the area would greatly enhance both safety and usability for residents. I kindly request that this issue be reviewed and that appropriate action be taken to ensure the road is properly maintained and safe for public use. Thank you for your attention to this matter. Kind Regrds, Gaetano.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 58, Mayplace Road West, Barnehurst, Bexleyheath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Used needle in the car park, 3rd May
item.description:
There is used needle in the car park , can you please remove it . It’s dangerous, dogs and children live in this area

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Mungo Park Way, St Mary Cray, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Loading bay sign removed, 3rd May
item.description:
Loading bay signe removed and now everyone is parking bothered and tesco lorry can't park or unload in swingate lane tesco. Cars parked in yellow lines and zige zage line causing a lot of problems

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Swingate Lane, Plumstead

item.title: Broken Rubbish Bag all over the pavement, 3rd May

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kirby Grove, The Borough, Greater London

item.title: Streatham Common North SW16 / Leigham Court Road, 3rd May
item.description:
Streatham Common North SW16 / Leigham Court Road junction A3 size flyposters attached to mainly pedestrian footpath guard rails all around this junction (plus may be traffic light poles etc) – polycarbonate boards secured with plastic zip ties so will need a knife and ‘snipes’ to remove.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Streatham Common North, West Norwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Leigham Court Road SW16 opposite side of, 3rd May
item.description:
Leigham Court Road SW16 opposite side of the road to house number 301 is a bus stop with a black square metal Lambeth bin next to it which is graffiti tagged. Please forward fixmystreet.com link to your contractor for reporting pin &, picture.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Leigham Court Road, West Norwood, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Entry of Falcon field allotments, 3rd May
item.description:
Domestic waste

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Church Road, Enfield
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Parked in a designated disabled bay for 6+ hours with no permission or notice, 3rd May
item.description:
This person has been parking her time and time again. He sees me waiting patiently in my car to avoid problems. I havent spoke to him as he acts quite hostile. He knowingly parks in my bay like its his home. Ive called the council multiple times but they either dont turn up or take over 3+ hours and by then he is usually gone. It is unacceptable and needs to stop. My mother struggles with mobility and the fact he is treating our personal space like this is selfish and disrespectful. Need a solution ASAP.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Queensway, Enfield
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bricks and debris on pavement from damaged wall, 3rd May
item.description:
Please could the damaged brickwork be removed from where it has fallen onto the pavement and the brick wall repaired. Thank you. Cllr Alisa Igoe

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Nichol Lane, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish left in garage area, 3rd May
item.description:
Rubbish in garage area You have taking garage back and put them out of use but you are creating a HEALTH AND SAFETY issue where you are not getting rid of rubbish and it is a FIRE RISK the same reason you closed all garages THIS MATTER NEEDS LOOKING IN TO URGENTLY

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Curtis Way, Thamesmead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Weeding Needed, 3rd May
item.description:
All along Argyle Way there are an extensive amount of weeds that need removing. Please note Argyle Way is an adopted highway both pavement and road. Please do not say it's private. I've had this discussion numerous times over 26 years.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Argyle Way, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road sign unreadable, 3rd May
item.description:
I have messaged twice before over this problem. The road sign for Hengrove Court is unreadable which is very dangerous for drivers looking for the close.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hengrove Court, Albany Park, Greater London
